mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity discount rates and risk appetite, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already above its recent 3-month average, because then the easing impulse is meaningful rather than noise.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not higher after negative 1-month changes in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips so that easing instances underperform tightening instances in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above the recent inflation trend raises the probability of tighter policy and a higher discount rate, which दब压 stocks over the following month. The effect should appear when inflation momentum is rising faster than the prior 3 months, not when CPI is merely level.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 3-month annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation accelerations do not precede below-median 20d SPX returns, or if the triggered sample produces positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combined rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals strengthening final demand and a healthier domestic business cycle, which supports equity earnings expectations over a one-quarter horizon. The mechanism fails if higher sales are accompanied by housing weakness, because then the growth signal is not broad-based.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positives in RSAFS and HOUST do not correspond to above-median 60d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply over a short window, labor-market deterioration signals slower future demand and weaker earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within a month. The signal is designed to activate only when unemployment is moving up, not when it is flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns after 0.3pp UNRATE rises are not below unconditional returns, or if the triggered sample has a positive mean return.

mechanism_narrative: An upside break in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and a better near-term earnings backdrop, which should support equities over the next few weeks. The effect is strongest when production is accelerating from an already weak base, because then the market is likely pricing a cyclical rebound.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if positive reversals in INDPRO fail to predict higher 20d SPX returns, or if the sign is opposite in most rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a fall in UNRATE captures genuine labor-market improvement that supports consumption and corporate revenue growth, producing positive SPX returns over the next month. This interaction should dominate single-series effects because payroll gains matter more when unemployment is also easing.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the joint condition does not yield positive excess 20d returns.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below its recent baseline signals demand weakening and prospective softness in cyclical activity, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal becomes meaningful only when housing starts are materially below their prior 6-month average.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its prior 6-month average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if large negative HOUST deviations do not precede below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the triggered sample mean is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real financing conditions and limits equity multiples, so forward S&P 500 returns should be negative over a month. The effect is strongest in tightening phases because markets then price slower growth and delayed easing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL above its prior 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the high-rate/high-inflation regime does not produce negative 20d SPX returns, or if the joint condition has no predictive edge relative to either series alone.
